items.headline: Mitch Marner's versatility big advantage for Vegas in 2026 playoffs
items.description:
LAS VEGAS -- What is Mitch Marner's role on the Vegas Golden Knights?
First-line left wing? Second-line center? Power-play quarterback? Penalty killer?
The answer is yes.
The 28-year-old has handled a lot from game to game and within games all season, while adjusting to a new team after playing his

items.headline: Boston Bruins second line responds in game 2 win against Buffalo Sabres
items.description:
BUFFALO -- Marco Sturm knew what he was doing.
The Boston Bruins coach did not beat around the bush after Game 1 of the Eastern Conference First Round against the Buffalo Sabres when he said he needed more from his second line, calling them “just OK” when pushed.

items.headline: Brandon Hagel enjoys bad guy role for Tampa Bay Lightning in Eastern First Round
items.description:
TAMPA -- The last time Brandon Hagel played a game with the magnitude of the Stanley Cup Playoffs at Bell Centre in Montreal, he instantly became a national hero in Canada.

items.headline: Denis Lemieux loving Montreal's 2026 playoff ride
items.description:
MONTREAL -- Broadcaster Jim Carr sits patiently on the set of his TV show "Sports Talk" and listens to Federal League Charlestown Chiefs goalie Denis Lemieux explain the rule of icing through his thick French-Canadian accent:

items.headline: NHL At the Rink Dallas Minnesota series will go 7 games Craig Button says
items.description:
Something will have gone wrong for the Dallas Stars and Minnesota Wild to not go to a Game 7 in their Western Conference First Round series, TSN broadcaster and former NHL general manager Craig Button said on this week's episode of the "NHL @TheRink" podcast.

items.headline: Trevor Lewis retires from NHL after 17 seasons
items.description:
Trevor Lewis announced his retirement from the NHL on Wednesday after 17 seasons.
The 39-year-old forward last played in the NHL in 2024-25 when he had 12 points (six goals, six assists) in 60 games with the Los Angeles Kings.
Lewis had 237 points (104 goals, 133 assists) in 1,034

items.headline: Fantasy hockey picks props projections for 2025-26 season
items.description: NHL.com identifies players to watch for free-to-play fantasy hockey games, props and futures throughout the 2026 Stanley Cup Playoffs. For more coverage, visit NHL.com/Fantasy.

items.headline: Los Angeles Kings need find way to win after Game 2 overtime loss
items.description:
DENVER -- Once again, the Los Angeles Kings played a tight game.
Once again, they held the mighty Colorado Avalanche offense in check.
Once again, they got a power-play goal.

items.headline: Los Angeles Kings Colorado Avalanche Game 2 recap April 21 2026
items.description: DENVER -- Nicolas Roy scored at 7:44 of overtime, and the Colorado Avalanche rallied to defeat the Los Angeles Kings 2-1 in Game 2 of the Western Conference First Round at Ball Arena on Tuesday.

items.headline: Utah Mammoth bringing momentum home after first Stanley Cup Playoff win
items.description:
LAS VEGAS -- All right, Utah. Get ready to get loud.
The Utah Mammoth earned their first Stanley Cup Playoff victory Tuesday when they defeated the Vegas Golden Knights 3-2 at T-Mobile Arena in Game 2 of the Western Conference First Round.
And so, they will host their first home

items.headline: Ukko-Pekka Luukkonen laments miscue for Buffalo Sabres in Game 2
items.description:
BUFFALO -- Ukko-Pekka Luukkonen didn’t shy away from the blame.
The Buffalo Sabres goalie put it on himself for not properly reading the puck when Boston Bruins forward Morgan Geekie flipped it on his backhand from center ice into the Buffalo zone.

items.headline: Utah Mammoth Vegas Golden Knights game 2 recap April 21 2026
items.description: LAS VEGAS -- Logan Cooley scored the go-ahead goal late in the third period to help the Utah Mammoth win their first Stanley Cup Playoff game in franchise history with a 3-2 victory against the Vegas Golden Knights in Game 2 of the Western Conference First Round at T-Mobile Arena

items.headline: Tampa Bay Lightning even series with wild and physical Game 2 victory
items.description:
TAMPA -- If the rest of the Eastern Conference First Round series between the Tampa Bay Lightning and Montreal Canadiens is like Game 2 on Tuesday, fans in each city are in for a treat.
And cardiologists in each city are in for a lot of business.
